note: Order matches v3 scenes: report (s15) before future/what's-next (s16).

lines.text: You see NeuraCard — a multimodal medical AI platform for clinical decision support.
The main problem is simple: one patient might need a brain scan, a heart tracing, an ultrasound, and a brain-wave recording — many separate tests.
Each test is read in isolation — different specialists, different machines, different schedules. No one ever sees the whole patient at once.
AI tools do exist for each test type. But today's models only do one thing — built for a single task, and rarely leaving the research lab.
Even worse, when a model gives a result, it cannot explain how it got there — a verdict with no reasoning that no clinician can really trust.
So the big picture never comes together, right when you need to decide. Data stays fragmented — and that means slower, less complete care.
NeuraCard solves this — one platform for every test type: Brain MRI, twelve-lead ECG, Echocardiogram, and EEG.
The workflow is simple. Open a patient, upload any test, and get an instant AI reading — all in one secure workspace, in seconds.
Start with Brain MRI. The platform detects the tumour and classifies its type, and shows you exactly where it looked. The evidence is shown, not hidden.
Switch to the twelve-lead ECG. You get the rhythm, the heart rate, and heart-rate variability — every finding flagged and explained, right beside the waveform.
In the Echocardiogram, you see the ejection fraction and chamber segmentation, measured straight from the echo loop, with the ultrasound frames beside it.
And for EEG, the platform reviews the brain-wave recording for harmful activity — multi-channel waveforms, key indicators, and a clear summary.
Throughout, NeuraCard is built for explainability. Grad-CAM and SHAP show exactly where each model focused, so a doctor checks the reasoning, not just the answer.
And it brings them together — findings from brain and heart, combined into one clear, readable interpretation.

Every test result then flows into one combined PDF report — imaging, signals, and interpretation, side by side. The whole picture, in a single document, ready for the doctor to read, sign, and act on.

Looking ahead, the roadmap is ambitious: learned multimodal fusion, to find brain-heart links no single test can show; prospective hospital trials on real patients; real-time monitoring at the bedside and on wearables; and full integration with hospital record systems — on the path to a certified clinical tool.
NeuraCard — every signal, together, for faster, more confident care. Thank you.
